prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a xav has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the xav-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a xav, a florp, and a fip. The xav is underneath the florp. The florp is to-the-left-of the fip. The xav has a purple button on top, a blue button on side, and a red button on bottom. The florp has a blue button on top and a purple button on bottom. The fip has a red button on top and a purple button on side. Pressing the purple button on the xav activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a scob, a arn, and a sploof. The sploof is underneath the arn. The arn is to-the-left-of the scob. The scob has a red button on top and a blue button on bottom. The arn has a purple button on top, a purple button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The sploof has a purple button on top, a blue button on side, and a red button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the xav-analog, and which button activates it?
